artifact.content_preview:
From: j.henderson@techsupplier.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:14:32 -0500

Alice,

Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation
with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre
migration.

Unfortunately, our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse
management system failure that has affected ou

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, making it impossible to meet the March 6 staging deadline and likely causing the Q2 migration timeline to slip.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line means there is no viable rapid alternative; the only fallback (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days, deepening the schedule impact and exposing the project to any additional supplier disruptions.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre, which is still ongoing. If the WMS issue is not fully resolved, further shipment disruptions beyond March 20 are possible, creating cascading schedule and budget risk.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Slippage of the March 8 migration window into Q2 may trigger budget overruns due to extended resource commitments (staff, contractors, facility access costs) and potential contractual penalties or re-procurement costs if the migration window cannot be rescheduled within the approved fiscal period.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: The staging and testing window (equipment required by March 6 for an March 8 migration) has been eliminated entirely by the delay. Even if equipment arrives March 20, there is no confirmed buffer for pre-migration validation, risking deployment of untested network infrastructure into a government data centre environment with potential compliance and operational continuity implications.
affected_stakeholders: Government of Canada IT security/compliance stakeholders

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.
